title: Conditions relating to solemnization of special marriages
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in any other law, a marriage between any two persons may be solemnized under this Act if the following conditions are fulfilled: neither party has a spouse living; neither party is incapable of giving valid consent; neither party is suffering from a mental disorder; the parties are not within degrees of prohibited relationship; and the male has completed 21 years and the female 18 years of age.

title: Notice of intended marriage
text: The parties intending to marry shall give written notice to the Marriage Officer of the district in which at least one of them has resided for not less than thirty days immediately preceding the date of notice.

title: Marriage notice book and publication
text: The Marriage Officer shall keep all such notices in a Marriage Notice Book and publish them by affixing a copy in a conspicuous place in his office.

title: Objection to marriage
text: Any person may object to the intended marriage on the ground that it contravenes one or more conditions specified in section 4. The Marriage Officer shall inquire into the objection within 30 days.

title: Procedure on receipt of objection
text: On receiving any objection, the Marriage Officer shall conduct an inquiry and make a decision. If satisfied that the objection is valid, he shall refuse to solemnize the marriage.

title: Divorce
text: A petition for divorce may be presented by either party to the marriage on grounds such as adultery, desertion for two years or more, cruelty, incurable unsoundness of mind, venereal disease, conversion to another religion, renunciation of the world, or not being heard alive for seven years. A wife may also seek divorce if the husband has committed rape, sodomy, or bestiality.

title: Divorce by mutual consent
text: Both parties may jointly file a petition for divorce by mutual consent if they have lived separately for one year or more and agree that the marriage has broken down irretrievably.

title: Restriction on petitions for divorce within one year of marriage
text: No petition for divorce shall be presented within one year of the marriage unless there are exceptional circumstances causing hardship.

title: Alimony and maintenance
text: The district court may, on application, order the payment of permanent alimony or maintenance to either spouse, considering the income and conduct of both parties.

title: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings
text: Either party may apply to the district court for maintenance during the pendency of legal proceedings, and the court may order the payment of such sums as it deems reasonable.

title: Custody of children
text: In any proceeding under this Act, the court may pass orders regarding custody, education, and maintenance of minor children, prioritizing their welfare.

title: Void marriages
text: Any marriage solemnized under this Act shall be void if it contravenes any condition specified in section 4, such as existing spouse, prohibited relationship, or incapacity to consent.

title: Voidable marriages
text: A marriage may be annulled as voidable if: it has not been consummated due to impotence; consent was obtained by fraud or coercion; or if the wife was pregnant by another person at the time of marriage.

title: Registration of marriages celebrated in other forms
text: Marriages solemnized in other forms may be registered under this Act upon mutual consent of both parties and fulfillment of conditions laid down in section 15.

title: Appeals from decrees and orders
text: Appeals against decrees and orders passed under this Act may be preferred to the appropriate appellate court within prescribed time limits.

title: Jurisdiction and procedure
text: All proceedings under this Act shall be governed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and the jurisdiction shall lie with the district courts having territorial authority over the parties.
